betterquesting:10.desc:8:
Well, who am I?
I am a scientist. I am curious, often to a fault. I am driven by a need for answers, one that can turn into obsession.
I am an inventor, a creative. Answers alone often lose meaningfulness to me if I cannot interface with them, if I cannot use them.
I am a fighter. A relentless fighter. I must fight for what I stand. 
I am hungry. The simple answers of everyday life are not enough for me, even when they maybe shoudl have been.
Love, family, those are the things I dicided to forget during my life as a scientist. My family, my friends, I wonder what they must have thought after I was gone, thinking I would be dead. 
"So passionate about experiments, probably fitting to meet death through an experiment."
The blood I am now spilling, the blood of this worm, it reflects who I am, not just as I see myself, not as others see me. My true self, one that, let's be honest, is often hard to love. 
And yet, I do accept this fact. That is who I am. I see it. I acknowledge it. I say it.
I say my Name. My True Name.
The language is one I have never heard, and yet it makes sense. It makes more sense than anything I have ever heard.
That is me, I recognize it. This Word, that is me.
